   566â†’func (c *Context) prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults refactoring.MoveResults, targets []addrs.Targetable) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   567â†’	if len(targets) < 1 {
   568â†’		return nil // the following only matters when targeting
   569â†’	}
   570â†’
   571â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   572â†’
   573â†’	var excluded []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
   574â†’	for _, result := range moveResults.Changes.Values() {
   575â†’		fromMatchesTarget := false
   576â†’		toMatchesTarget := false
   577â†’		for _, targetAddr := range targets {
   578â†’			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.From) {
   579â†’				fromMatchesTarget = true
   580â†’			}
   581â†’			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.To) {
   582â†’				toMatchesTarget = true
   583â†’			}
   584â†’		}
   585â†’		if !fromMatchesTarget {
   586â†’			excluded = append(excluded, result.From)
   587â†’		}
   588â†’		if !toMatchesTarget {
   589â†’			excluded = append(excluded, result.To)
   590â†’		}
   591â†’	}
   592â†’	if len(excluded) > 0 {
   593â†’		sort.Slice(excluded, func(i, j int) bool {
   594â†’			return excluded[i].Less(excluded[j])
   595â†’		})
   596â†’
   597â†’		var listBuf strings.Builder
   598â†’		var prevResourceAddr addrs.AbsResource
   599â†’		for _, instAddr := range excluded {
   600â†’			// Targeting generally ends up selecting whole resources rather
   601â†’			// than individual instances, because we don't factor in
   602â†’			// individual instances until DynamicExpand, so we're going to
   603â†’			// always show whole resource addresses here, excluding any
   604â†’			// instance keys. (This also neatly avoids dealing with the
   605â†’			// different quoting styles required for string instance keys
   606â†’			// on different shells, which is handy.)
   607â†’			//
   608â†’			// To avoid showing duplicates when we have multiple instances
   609â†’			// of the same resource, we'll remember the most recent
   610â†’			// resource we rendered in prevResource, which is sufficient
   611â†’			// because we sorted the list of instance addresses above, and
   612â†’			// our sort order always groups together instances of the same
   613â†’			// resource.
   614â†’			resourceAddr := instAddr.ContainingResource()
   615â†’			if resourceAddr.Equal(prevResourceAddr) {
   616â†’				continue
   617â†’			}
   618â†’			fmt.Fprintf(&listBuf, "\n  -target=%q", resourceAddr.String())
   619â†’			prevResourceAddr = resourceAddr
   620â†’		}
   621â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   622â†’			tfdiags.Error,
   623â†’			"Moved resource instances excluded by targeting",
   624â†’			fmt.Sprintf(
   625â†’				"Resource instances in your current state have moved to new addresses in the latest configuration. Terraform must include those resource instances while planning in order to ensure a correct result, but your -target=... options do not fully cover all of those resource instances.\n\nTo create a valid plan, either remove your -target=... options altogether or add the following additional target options:%s\n\nNote that adding these options may include further additional resource instances in your plan, in order to respect object dependencies.",
   626â†’				listBuf.String(),
   627â†’			),
   628â†’		))
   629â†’	}
   630â†’
   631â†’	return diags
   632â†’}

   652â†’func (c *Context) findForgetTargets(config *configs.Config) (forgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource, forgetModules []addrs.Module, diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   653â†’	removeStmts, diags := refactoring.FindRemoveStatements(config)

   673â†’func (c *Context) planWalk(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   674â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   675â†’	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking plan graph for %s", opts.Mode)
   676â†’
   677â†’	prevRunState = prevRunState.DeepCopy() // don't modify the caller's object when we process the moves
   678â†’	moveStmts, moveResults, moveDiags := c.prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config, prevRunState)
   679â†’	diags = diags.Append(moveDiags)
   680â†’	if moveDiags.HasErrors() {
   681â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   682â†’	}
   683â†’
   684â†’	// If resource targeting is in effect then it might conflict with the
   685â†’	// move result.
   686â†’	diags = diags.Append(c.prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults, opts.Targets))
   687â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   688â†’		// We'll return early here, because if we have any moved resource
   689â†’		// instances excluded by targeting then planning is likely to encounter
   690â†’		// strange problems that may lead to confusing error messages.
   691â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   692â†’	}
   693â†’
   694â†’	graph, walkOp, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
   695â†’	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   696â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   697â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   698â†’	}
   699â†’
   700â†’	timestamp := time.Now().UTC()
   701â†’	if opts.ForcePlanTimestamp != nil {
   702â†’		// Some tests use this to produce stable results to assert against.
   703â†’		timestamp = *opts.ForcePlanTimestamp
   704â†’	}
   705â†’
   706â†’	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   707â†’	if opts != nil {
   708â†’		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   709â†’	}
   710â†’
   711â†’	// If we get here then we should definitely have a non-nil "graph", which
   712â†’	// we can now walk.
   713â†’	changes := plans.NewChanges()
   714â†’
   715â†’	// Initialize the results table to validate provider function calls.
   716â†’	// Hold reference to this so we can store the table data in the plan file.
   717â†’	providerFuncResults := providers.NewFunctionResultsTable(nil)
   718â†’
   719â†’	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, walkOp, &graphWalkOpts{
   720â†’		Config:                     config,
   721â†’		InputState:                 prevRunState,
   722â†’		ExternalProviderConfigs:    externalProviderConfigs,
   723â†’		DeferralAllowed:            opts.DeferralAllowed,
   724â†’		ExternalDependencyDeferred: opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred,
   725â†’		Changes:                    changes,
   726â†’		MoveResults:                moveResults,
   727â†’		Overrides:                  opts.Overrides,
   728â†’		PlanTimeTimestamp:          timestamp,
   729â†’		ProviderFuncResults:        providerFuncResults,
   730â†’		Forget:                     opts.Forget,
   731â†’	})
   732â†’	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
   733â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   734â†’
   735â†’	allInsts := walker.InstanceExpander.AllInstances()
   736â†’
   737â†’	moveValidateDiags := c.postPlanValidateMoves(config, moveStmts, allInsts)
   738â†’	if moveValidateDiags.HasErrors() {
   739â†’		// If any of the move statements are invalid then those errors take
   740â†’		// precedence over any other errors because an incomplete move graph
   741â†’		// is quite likely to be the _cause_ of various errors. This oddity
   742â†’		// comes from the fact that we need to apply the moves before we
   743â†’		// actually validate them, because validation depends on the result

   749â†’	if moveResults.Blocked.Len() > 0 && !diags.HasErrors() {

   759â†’	// representation of the subset of changes we were able to plan before
   760â†’	// we encountered errors, which we'll return as part of a non-nil plan
   761â†’	// so that e.g. the UI can show what was planned so far in case that extra
   762â†’	// context helps the user to understand the error messages we're returning.

   765â†’	// The refreshed state may have data resource objects which were deferred
   766â†’	// to apply and cannot be serialized.
   767â†’	walker.RefreshState.RemovePlannedResourceInstanceObjects()
   768â†’	priorState := walker.RefreshState.Close()
   769â†’
   770â†’	driftedResources, driftDiags := c.driftedResources(config, prevRunState, priorState, moveResults)
   771â†’	diags = diags.Append(driftDiags)
   772â†’
   773â†’	deferredResources, deferredDiags := c.deferredResources(config, walker.Deferrals.GetDeferredChanges(), priorState)
   774â†’	diags = diags.Append(deferredDiags)
   775â†’
   776â†’	var forgottenResources []string
   777â†’	for _, rc := range changes.Resources {
   778â†’		if rc.Action == plans.Forget {
   779â†’			// TODO KEM display resource ids
   780â†’			forgottenResources = append(forgottenResources, fmt.Sprintf(" - %s", rc.Addr))
   781â†’		}
   782â†’	}
   783â†’	if len(forgottenResources) > 0 {
   784â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   785â†’			tfdiags.Warning,
   786â†’			"Some objects will no longer be managed by Terraform",
   787â†’			fmt.Sprintf("If you apply this plan, Terraform will discard its tracking information for the following objects, but it will not delete them:\n%s\n\nAfter applying this plan, Terraform will no longer manage these objects. You will need to import them into Terraform to manage them again.", strings.Join(forgottenResources, "\n")),
   788â†’		))
   789â†’	}
   790â†’	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, prevRunState)
   791â†’	// We must finish building a plan object, and cannot return early here.
   792â†’	var changesSrc *plans.ChangesSrc
   793â†’	var err error
   794â†’	if !schemaDiags.HasErrors() {
   795â†’		changesSrc, err = changes.Encode(schemas)
   796â†’		if err != nil {
   797â†’			diags = diags.Append(err)
   798â†’		}
   799â†’	}
   800â†’
   801â†’	plan := &plans.Plan{
   802â†’		UIMode:                  opts.Mode,
   803â†’		Changes:                 changesSrc,
   804â†’		DriftedResources:        driftedResources,
   805â†’		DeferredResources:       deferredResources,
   806â†’		PrevRunState:            prevRunState,
   807â†’		PriorState:              priorState,
   808â†’		ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   809â†’		Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   810â†’		Checks:                  states.NewCheckResults(walker.Checks),
   811â†’		Timestamp:               timestamp,
   812â†’		ProviderFunctionResults: providerFuncResults.GetHashes(),
   813â†’
   814â†’		// Other fields get populated by Context.Plan after we return
   815â†’	}
   816â†’
   817â†’	// Our final rulings on whether the plan is "complete" and "applyable".
   818â†’	// See the documentation for these plan fields to learn what exactly they
   819â†’	// are intended to mean.
   820â†’	if !diags.HasErrors() {
   821â†’		if len(opts.Targets) == 0 && !walker.Deferrals.HaveAnyDeferrals() {
   822â†’			// A plan without any targets or deferred actions should be
   823â†’			// complete if we didn't encounter errors while producing it.
   824â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is complete")
   825â†’			plan.Complete = true
   826â†’		} else {
   827â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is incomplete")
   828â†’		}
   829â†’		if opts.Mode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   830â†’			// In refresh-only mode we explicitly don't expect to propose any
   831â†’			// actions, but the plan is applyable if the state was changed
   832â†’			// in an interesting way by the refresh step.
   833â†’			plan.Applyable = !plan.PriorState.ManagedResourcesEqual(plan.PrevRunState) || !plan.PriorState.RootOutputValuesEqual(plan.PrevRunState)
   834â†’		} else {
   835â†’			// For other planning modes a plan is applyable if its "changes"
   836â†’			// are not considered empty (by whatever rules the plans package
   837â†’			// uses to decide that).
   838â†’			plan.Applyable = !plan.Changes.Empty()
   839â†’		}
   840â†’		if plan.Applyable {
   841â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is applyable")
   842â†’		} else {
   843â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is not applyable")
   844â†’		}
   845â†’	} else {
   846â†’		log.Println("[WARN] Planning encountered errors, so plan is not applyable")
   847â†’	}
   848â†’
   849â†’	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
   850â†’	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
   851â†’	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
   852â†’	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   853â†’
   854â†’	return plan, evalScope, diags
   855â†’}

   857â†’func (c *Context) deferredResources(config *configs.Config, deferrals []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChange, state *states.State) ([]*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {

   917â†’			GenerateConfigPath:      opts.GenerateConfigPath,
   918â†’		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   919â†’		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   920â†’	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   921â†’		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   922â†’			Config:                  config,
   923â†’			State:                   prevRunState,
   924â†’			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   925â†’			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   926â†’			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   927â†’			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   928â†’			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   929â†’			skipPlanChanges:         true, // this activates "refresh only" mode.
   930â†’			Operation:               walkPlan,
   931â†’			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   932â†’			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   933â†’		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   934â†’		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   935â†’	case plans.DestroyMode:
   936â†’		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   937â†’			Config:                  config,
   938â†’			State:                   prevRunState,
   939â†’			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   940â†’			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   941â†’			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   942â†’			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   943â†’			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   944â†’			Operation:               walkPlanDestroy,
   945â†’			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,

   954â†’// driftedResources is a best-effort attempt to compare the current and prior
   955â†’// state. If we cannot decode the prior state for some reason, this should only
   956â†’// return warnings to help the user correlate any missing resources in the
   957â†’// report. This is known to happen when targeting a subset of resources,
   958â†’// because the excluded instances will have been removed from the plan and
   959â†’// not upgraded.
   960â†’func (c *Context) driftedResources(config *configs.Config, oldState, newState *states.State, moves refactoring.MoveResults) ([]*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   961â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   962â†’
   963â†’	if newState.ManagedResourcesEqual(oldState) && moves.Changes.Len() == 0 {
   964â†’		// Nothing to do, because we only detect and report drift for managed
   965â†’		// resource instances.
   966â†’		return nil, diags
   967â†’	}
   968â†’
   969â†’	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, newState)
   970â†’	diags = diags.Append(schemaDiags)
   971â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   972â†’		return nil, diags
   973â†’	}
   974â†’
   975â†’	var drs []*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc
   976â†’
   977â†’	for _, ms := range oldState.Modules {
   978â†’		for _, rs := range ms.Resources {
   979â†’			if rs.Addr.Resource.Mode != addrs.ManagedResourceMode {
   980â†’				// Drift reporting is only for managed resources
   981â†’				continue
   982â†’			}
   983â†’
   984â†’			provider := rs.ProviderConfig.Provider
   985â†’			for key, oldIS := range rs.Instances {
   986â†’				if oldIS.Current == nil {
   987â†’					// Not interested in instances that only have deposed objects
   988â†’					continue
   989â†’				}
   990â†’				addr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)
   991â†’
   992â†’				// Previous run address defaults to the current address, but
   993â†’				// can differ if the resource moved before refreshing
   994â†’				prevRunAddr := addr
   995â†’				if move, ok := moves.Changes.GetOk(addr); ok {
   996â†’					prevRunAddr = move.From
   997â†’				}
   998â†’
   999â†’				newIS := newState.ResourceInstance(addr)
  1000â†’
  1001â†’				schema, _ := schemas.ResourceTypeConfig(
  1002â†’					provider,
  1003â†’					addr.Resource.Resource.Mode,
  1004â†’					addr.Resource.Resource.Type,
  1005â†’				)
  1006â†’				if schema == nil {
  1007â†’					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1008â†’						tfdiags.Warning,
  1009â†’						"Missing resource schema from provider",
  1010â†’						fmt.Sprintf("No resource schema found for %s when decoding prior state", addr.Resource.Resource.Type),
  1011â†’					))
  1012â†’					continue
  1013â†’				}
  1014â†’				ty := schema.ImpliedType()
  1015â†’
  1016â†’				oldObj, err := oldIS.Current.Decode(ty)
  1017â†’				if err != nil {
  1018â†’					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1019â†’						tfdiags.Warning,
  1020â†’						"Failed to decode resource from state",
  1021â†’						fmt.Sprintf("Error decoding %q from prior state: %s", addr.String(), err),
  1022â†’					))
  1023â†’					continue
  1024â†’				}
  1025â†’
  1026â†’				var newObj *states.ResourceInstanceObject
  1027â†’				if newIS != nil && newIS.Current != nil {
  1028â†’					newObj, err = newIS.Current.Decode(ty)
  1029â†’					if err != nil {
  1030â†’						diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
  1031â†’							tfdiags.Warning,
  1032â†’							"Failed to decode resource from state",
  1033â†’							fmt.Sprintf("Error decoding %q from prior state: %s", addr.String(), err),
  1034â†’						))
  1035â†’						continue
  1036â†’					}
  1037â†’				}
  1038â†’
  1039â†’				var oldVal, newVal cty.Value
  1040â†’				oldVal = oldObj.Value
  1041â†’				if newObj != nil {
  1042â†’					newVal = newObj.Value
  1043â†’				} else {
  1044â†’					newVal = cty.NullVal(ty)
  1045â†’				}
  1046â†’
  1047â†’				if oldVal.RawEquals(newVal) && addr.Equal(prevRunAddr) {
  1048â†’					// No drift if the two values are semantically equivalent
  1049â†’					// and no move has happened
  1050â†’					continue
  1051â†’				}
  1052â†’
  1053â†’				// We can detect three types of changes after refreshing state,
  1054â†’				// only two of which are easily understood as "drift":
  1055â†’				//
  1056â†’				// - Resources which were deleted outside of Terraform;
  1057â†’				// - Resources where the object value has changed outside of
  1058â†’				//   Terraform;
  1059â†’				// - Resources which have been moved without other changes.
  1060â†’				//
  1061â†’				// All of these are returned as drift, to allow refresh-only plans
  1062â†’				// to present a full set of changes which will be applied.
  1063â†’				var action plans.Action
  1064â†’				switch {
  1065â†’				case newVal.IsNull():
  1066â†’					action = plans.Delete
  1067â†’				case !oldVal.RawEquals(newVal):
  1068â†’					action = plans.Update
  1069â†’				default:
  1070â†’					action = plans.NoOp
  1071â†’				}
  1072â†’
  1073â†’				change := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{
  1074â†’					Addr:         addr,
  1075â†’					PrevRunAddr:  prevRunAddr,
  1076â†’					ProviderAddr: rs.ProviderConfig,
  1077â†’					Change: plans.Change{
  1078â†’						Action: action,
  1079â†’						Before: oldVal,
  1080â†’						After:  newVal,
  1081â†’					},
  1082â†’				}
  1083â†’
  1084â†’				changeSrc, err := change.Encode(ty)
  1085â†’				if err != nil {
  1086â†’					diags = diags.Append(err)
  1087â†’					return nil, diags
  1088â†’				}
  1089â†’
  1090â†’				drs = append(drs, changeSrc)
  1091â†’			}
  1092â†’		}
  1093â†’	}
  1094â†’
  1095â†’	return drs, diags
  1096â†’}

  1098â†’// PlanGraphForUI is a last vestage of graphs in the public interface of Context
  1099â†’// (as opposed to graphs as an implementation detail) intended only for use
  1100â†’// by the "terraform graph" command when asked to render a plan-time graph.
  1101â†’//
  1102â†’// The result of this is intended only for rendering to the user as a dot
  1103â†’// graph, and so may change in future in order to make the result more useful
  1104â†’// in that context, even if drifts away from the physical graph that Terraform
  1105â†’// Core currently uses as an implementation detail of planning.
  1106â†’func (c *Context) PlanGraphForUI(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, mode plans.Mode) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
  1107â†’	// For now though, this really is just the internal graph, confusing
  1108â†’	// implementation details and all.
  1109â†’
  1110â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
  1111â†’
  1112â†’	opts := &PlanOpts{Mode: mode}
  1113â†’
  1114â†’	graph, _, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
  1115â†’	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
  1116â†’	return graph, diags
  1117â†’}

If Terraform's proposed changes aren't appropriate, you must first resolve the conflicts using the \"terraform state\" subcommands and then create a new plan.",

   140â†’// Plan generates an execution plan by comparing the given configuration
   141â†’// with the given previous run state.
   142â†’//
   143â†’// The given planning options allow control of various other details of the
   144â†’// planning process that are not represented directly in the configuration.
   145â†’// You can use terraform.DefaultPlanOpts to generate a normal plan with no
   146â†’// special options.
   147â†’//
   148â†’// If the returned diagnostics contains no errors then the returned plan is
   149â†’// applyable, although Terraform cannot guarantee that applying it will fully
   150â†’// succeed. If the returned diagnostics contains errors but this method
   151â†’// still returns a non-nil Plan then the plan describes the subset of actions
   152â†’// planned so far, which is not safe to apply but could potentially be used
   153â†’// by the UI layer to give extra context to support understanding of the
   154â†’// returned error messages.
   155â†’func (c *Context) Plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   156â†’	plan, _, diags := c.PlanAndEval(config, prevRunState, opts)
   157â†’	return plan, diags
   158â†’}

   160â†’// PlanAndEval is like [Context.Plan] except that it additionally makes a
   161â†’// best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the
   162â†’// root module based on the content of the generated plan.
   163â†’//
   164â†’// The scope will be nil if the planning process doesn't complete successfully
   165â†’// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned plan is nil
   166â†’// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
   167â†’// to be nil even when the plan isn't, if the plan is not complete enough for
   168â†’// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.
   169â†’func (c *Context) PlanAndEval(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   170â†’	defer c.acquireRun("plan")()
   171â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   172â†’
   173â†’	// Save the downstream functions from needing to deal with these broken situations.
   174â†’	// No real callers should rely on these, but we have a bunch of old and
   175â†’	// sloppy tests that don't always populate arguments properly.

   182â†’	if opts == nil {

   193â†’	// If required dependencies are not available then we'll bail early since

   229â†’	}

   232â†’		// upgrade to be "replace", so doesn't make sense to combine those.

   234â†’			tfdiags.Error,
   235â†’			"Unsupported plan mode",
   236â†’			"Forcing resource instance replacement (with -replace=...) is allowed only in normal planning mode.",
   237â†’		))

   239â†’	}

   243â†’			"Unsupported plan mode",
   244â†’			"Forgetting all resources is only allowed in the context of a destroy plan. This is a bug in Terraform, please report it.",
   245â†’		))
   246â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   247â†’	}
   248â†’
   249â†’	// By the time we get here, we should have values defined for all of
   250â†’	// the root module variables, even if some of them are "unknown". It's the
   251â†’	// caller's responsibility to have already handled the decoding of these
   252â†’	// from the various ways the CLI allows them to be set and to produce

   254â†’	// the error message from checkInputVariables should never be seen and
   255â†’	// includes language asking the user to report a bug.
   256â†’	varDiags := checkInputVariables(config.Module.Variables, opts.SetVariables)
   257â†’	diags = diags.Append(varDiags)
   258â†’
   259â†’	if len(opts.Targets) > 0 {
   260â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   261â†’			tfdiags.Warning,
   262â†’			"Resource targeting is in effect",
   263â†’			`You are creating a plan with the -target option, which means that the result of this plan may not represent all of the changes requested by the current configuration.
   264â†’

   267â†’	}

   269â†’	var plan *plans.Plan
   270â†’	var planDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   271â†’	var evalScope *lang.Scope
   272â†’	switch opts.Mode {

   274â†’		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.plan(config, prevRunState, opts)
   275â†’	case plans.DestroyMode:

   288â†’	// convert the variables into the format expected for the plan
   289â†’	varVals := make(map[string]plans.DynamicValue, len(opts.SetVariables))
   290â†’	varMarks := make(map[string][]cty.PathValueMarks, len(opts.SetVariables))
   291â†’	applyTimeVariables := collections.NewSetCmp[string]()
   292â†’	for k, iv := range opts.SetVariables {
   293â†’		// If any input variables were declared as ephemeral and set to a
   294â†’		// non-null value then those variables must be provided again (possibly
   295â†’		// with _different_ non-null values) during the apply phase.

   298â†’			// in the named values state, rather than the value as provided
   299â†’			// by the caller, so we can take into account the transforms

   301â†’			// point for now, though.
   302â†’			if iv.Value != cty.NilVal && !iv.Value.IsNull() {
   303â†’				applyTimeVariables.Add(k)

   306â†’		}
   307â†’
   308â†’		// Non-ephemeral variables must remain unchanged between plan and
   309â†’		// apply, so we'll record their actual values.

   311â†’			continue // We only record values that the caller actually set
   312â†’		}
   313â†’
   314â†’		// Root variable values arriving from the traditional CLI path are
   315â†’		// unmarked, as they are directly decoded from .tfvars, CLI arguments,
   316â†’		// or the environment. However, variable values arriving from other
   317â†’		// plans (via the coordination efforts of the stacks runtime) may have
   318â†’		// gathered marks during evaluation. We must separate the value from

   320â†’		// which cannot represent marks.
   321â†’		value, pvm := iv.Value.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
   322â†’
   323â†’		// We use cty.DynamicPseudoType here so that we'll save both the
   324â†’		// value _and_ its dynamic type in the plan, so we can recover
   325â†’		// exactly the same value later.
   326â†’		dv, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(value, cty.DynamicPseudoType)

   328â†’			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   329â†’				tfdiags.Error,
   330â†’				"Failed to prepare variable value for plan",
   331â†’				fmt.Sprintf("The value for variable %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", k, err),
   332â†’			))

   334â†’		}

   336â†’		varMarks[k] = pvm
   337â†’	}
   338â†’
   339â†’	// insert the run-specific data from the context into the plan; variables,
   340â†’	// targets and provider SHAs.
   341â†’	if plan != nil {
   342â†’		plan.VariableValues = varVals

   346â†’		}

   350â†’	}
   351â†’
   352â†’	if plan != nil {
   353â†’		relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, plan)
   354â†’		diags = diags.Append(rDiags)

   356â†’	}

   358â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   359â†’		// We can't proceed further with an invalid plan, because an invalid
   360â†’		// plan isn't applyable by definition.
   361â†’		if plan != nil {

   363â†’			// be accidentally applied even though it's incomplete.
   364â†’			plan.Errored = true
   365â†’		}
   366â†’		return plan, evalScope, diags
   367â†’	}
   368â†’
   369â†’	diags = diags.Append(c.checkApplyGraph(plan, config, opts))
   370â†’
   371â†’	return plan, evalScope, diags
   372â†’}
   373â†’
   374â†’// checkApplyGraph builds the apply graph out of the current plan to
   375â†’// check for any errors that may arise once the planned changes are added to
   376â†’// the graph. This allows terraform to report errors (mostly cycles) during
   377â†’// plan that would otherwise only crop up during apply
   378â†’func (c *Context) checkApplyGraph(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *PlanOpts) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   379â†’	if plan.Changes.Empty() {
   380â†’		log.Println("[DEBUG] no planned changes, skipping apply graph check")
   381â†’		return nil
   382â†’	}
   383â†’	log.Println("[DEBUG] building apply graph to check for errors")
   384â†’
   385â†’	_, _, diags := c.applyGraph(plan, config, opts.ApplyOpts(), true)

   388â†’
   389â†’var DefaultPlanOpts = &PlanOpts{
   390â†’	Mode: plans.NormalMode,
   391â†’}
   392â†’
   393â†’// SimplePlanOpts is a constructor to help with creating "simple" values of
   394â†’// PlanOpts which only specify a mode and input variables.
   395â†’//
   396â†’// This helper function is primarily intended for use in straightforward
   397â†’// tests that don't need any of the more "esoteric" planning options. For

   441â†’	// to refresh only, the set of resource changes should always be empty.

   468â†’func (c *Context) destroyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {

   477â†’	// A destroy plan starts by running Refresh to read any pending data
   478â†’	// sources, and remove missing managed resources. This is required because
   479â†’	// a "destroy plan" is only creating delete changes, and is essentially a
   480â†’	// local operation.

   484â†’	// resource type schemas, so NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance.Execute

   486â†’	// _is_ set. This coupling between the two is unfortunate but necessary
   487â†’	// to work within our current structure.

   495â†’		// the state and configuration may not match during a destroy, and a
   496â†’		// normal refresh plan can fail with evaluation errors. In the future
   497â†’		// the destroy plan should take care of refreshing instances itself,
   498â†’		// where the special cases of evaluation and skipping condition checks
   499â†’		// can be done.
   500â†’		refreshPlan, _, refreshDiags := c.plan(config, prevRunState, &refreshOpts)
   501â†’		if refreshDiags.HasErrors() {
   502â†’			// NOTE: Normally we'd append diagnostics regardless of whether
   503â†’			// there are errors, just in case there are warnings we'd want to
   504â†’			// preserve, but we're intentionally _not_ doing that here because
   505â†’			// if the first plan succeeded then we'll be running another plan
   506â†’			// in DestroyMode below, and we don't want to double-up any
   507â†’			// warnings that both plan walks would generate.
   508â†’			// (This does mean we won't show any warnings that would've been
   509â†’			// unique to only this walk, but we're assuming here that if the
   510â†’			// warnings aren't also applicable to a destroy plan then we'd
   511â†’			// rather not show them here, because this non-destroy plan for
   512â†’			// refreshing is largely an implementation detail.)
   513â†’			diags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)
   514â†’			return nil, nil, diags
   515â†’		}
   516â†’
   517â†’		// We'll use the refreshed state -- which is the  "prior state" from
   518â†’		// the perspective of this "destroy plan" -- as the starting state
   519â†’		// for our destroy-plan walk, so it can take into account if we
   520â†’		// detected during refreshing that anything was already deleted outside
   521â†’		// of Terraform.
   522â†’		priorState = refreshPlan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
   523â†’
   524â†’		// The refresh plan may have upgraded state for some resources, make
   525â†’		// sure we store the new version.
   526â†’		prevRunState = refreshPlan.PrevRunState.DeepCopy()
   527â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: now _really_ creating a destroy plan")
   528â†’	}
   529â†’
   530â†’	destroyPlan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, priorState, opts)
   531â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   532â†’	if walkDiags.HasErrors() {
   533â†’		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   534â†’		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   535â†’		// to help understand the errors.
   536â†’		return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
   537â†’	}
   538â†’
   539â†’	if !opts.SkipRefresh {
   540â†’		// If we didn't skip refreshing then we want the previous run state to
   541â†’		// be the one we originally fed into the c.refreshOnlyPlan call above,
   542â†’		// not the refreshed version we used for the destroy planWalk.
   543â†’		destroyPlan.PrevRunState = prevRunState
   544â†’	}
   545â†’
   546â†’	relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, destroyPlan)
   547â†’	diags = diags.Append(rDiags)
   548â†’
   549â†’	destroyPlan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs
   550â†’	return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
   551â†’}

   553â†’func (c *Context) prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State) ([]refactoring.MoveStatement, refactoring.MoveResults, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   554â†’	explicitMoveStmts := refactoring.FindMoveStatements(config)
   555â†’	implicitMoveStmts := refactoring.ImpliedMoveStatements(config, prevRunState, explicitMoveStmts)
   556â†’	var moveStmts []refactoring.MoveStatement
   557â†’	if stmtsLen := len(explicitMoveStmts) + len(implicitMoveStmts); stmtsLen > 0 {
   558â†’		moveStmts = make([]refactoring.MoveStatement, 0, stmtsLen)
   559â†’		moveStmts = append(moveStmts, explicitMoveStmts...)
   560â†’		moveStmts = append(moveStmts, implicitMoveStmts...)
   561â†’	}
   562â†’	moveResults, diags := refactoring.ApplyMoves(moveStmts, prevRunState, c.plugins.ProviderFactories())
   563â†’	return moveStmts, moveResults, diags
   564â†’}

   566â†’func (c *Context) prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults refactoring.MoveResults, targets []addrs.Targetable) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   567â†’	if len(targets) < 1 {
   568â†’		return nil // the following only matters when targeting
   569â†’	}
   570â†’
   571â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   572â†’
   573â†’	var excluded []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
   574â†’	for _, result := range moveResults.Changes.Values() {
   575â†’		fromMatchesTarget := false
   576â†’		toMatchesTarget := false
   577â†’		for _, targetAddr := range targets {
   578â†’			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.From) {
   579â†’				fromMatchesTarget = true
   580â†’			}
   581â†’			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.To) {
   582â†’				toMatchesTarget = true
   583â†’			}
   584â†’		}
   585â†’		if !fromMatchesTarget {
   586â†’			excluded = append(excluded, result.From)
   587â†’		}
   588â†’		if !toMatchesTarget {
   589â†’			excluded = append(excluded, result.To)
   590â†’		}
   591â†’	}
   592â†’	if len(excluded) > 0 {
   593â†’		sort.Slice(excluded, func(i, j int) bool {
   594â†’			return excluded[i].Less(excluded[j])
   595â†’		})
   596â†’
   597â†’		var listBuf strings.Builder
   598â†’		var prevResourceAddr addrs.AbsResource
   599â†’		for _, instAddr := range excluded {
   600â†’			// Targeting generally ends up selecting whole resources rather
   601â†’			// than individual instances, because we don't factor in
   602â†’			// individual instances until DynamicExpand, so we're going to
   603â†’			// always show whole resource addresses here, excluding any
   604â†’			// instance keys. (This also neatly avoids dealing with the
   605â†’			// different quoting styles required for string instance keys
   606â†’			// on different shells, which is handy.)
   607â†’			//
   608â†’			// To avoid showing duplicates when we have multiple instances
   609â†’			// of the same resource, we'll remember the most recent
   610â†’			// resource we rendered in prevResource, which is sufficient
   611â†’			// because we sorted the list of instance addresses above, and
   612â†’			// our sort order always groups together instances of the same
   613â†’			// resource.
   614â†’			resourceAddr := instAddr.ContainingResource()
   615â†’			if resourceAddr.Equal(prevResourceAddr) {
   616â†’				continue
   617â†’			}
   618â†’			fmt.Fprintf(&listBuf, "\n  -target=%q", resourceAddr.String())
   619â†’			prevResourceAddr = resourceAddr
   620â†’		}
   621â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   622â†’			tfdiags.Error,
   623â†’			"Moved resource instances excluded by targeting",
   624â†’			fmt.Sprintf(
   625â†’				"Resource instances in your current state have moved to new addresses in the latest configuration. Terraform must include those resource instances while planning in order to ensure a correct result, but your -target=... options do not fully cover all of those resource instances.\n\nTo create a valid plan, either remove your -target=... options altogether or add the following additional target options:%s\n\nNote that adding these options may include further additional resource instances in your plan, in order to respect object dependencies.",
   626â†’				listBuf.String(),
   627â†’			),
   628â†’		))
   629â†’	}
   630â†’
   631â†’	return diags
   632â†’}

   635â†’	return refactoring.ValidateMoves(stmts, config, allInsts)
   636â†’}

   653â†’	removeStmts, diags := refactoring.FindRemoveStatements(config)

   673â†’func (c *Context) planWalk(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   674â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   675â†’	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking plan graph for %s", opts.Mode)
   676â†’
   677â†’	prevRunState = prevRunState.DeepCopy() // don't modify the caller's object when we process the moves
   678â†’	moveStmts, moveResults, moveDiags := c.prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config, prevRunState)
   679â†’	diags = diags.Append(moveDiags)
   680â†’	if moveDiags.HasErrors() {
   681â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   682â†’	}
   683â†’
   684â†’	// If resource targeting is in effect then it might conflict with the
   685â†’	// move result.
   686â†’	diags = diags.Append(c.prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults, opts.Targets))
   687â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   688â†’		// We'll return early here, because if we have any moved resource
   689â†’		// instances excluded by targeting then planning is likely to encounter
   690â†’		// strange problems that may lead to confusing error messages.
   691â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   692â†’	}
   693â†’
   694â†’	graph, walkOp, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
   695â†’	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   696â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   697â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   698â†’	}
   699â†’
   700â†’	timestamp := time.Now().UTC()
   701â†’	if opts.ForcePlanTimestamp != nil {
   702â†’		// Some tests use this to produce stable results to assert against.
   703â†’		timestamp = *opts.ForcePlanTimestamp
   704â†’	}
   705â†’
   706â†’	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   707â†’	if opts != nil {
   708â†’		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   709â†’	}
   710â†’
   711â†’	// If we get here then we should definitely have a non-nil "graph", which
   712â†’	// we can now walk.
   713â†’	changes := plans.NewChanges()
   714â†’
   715â†’	// Initialize the results table to validate provider function calls.
   716â†’	// Hold reference to this so we can store the table data in the plan file.
   717â†’	providerFuncResults := providers.NewFunctionResultsTable(nil)
   718â†’
   719â†’	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, walkOp, &graphWalkOpts{
   720â†’		Config:                     config,
   721â†’		InputState:                 prevRunState,
   722â†’		ExternalProviderConfigs:    externalProviderConfigs,
   723â†’		DeferralAllowed:            opts.DeferralAllowed,
   724â†’		ExternalDependencyDeferred: opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred,
   725â†’		Changes:                    changes,
   726â†’		MoveResults:                moveResults,
   727â†’		Overrides:                  opts.Overrides,
   728â†’		PlanTimeTimestamp:          timestamp,
   729â†’		ProviderFuncResults:        providerFuncResults,
   730â†’		Forget:                     opts.Forget,
   731â†’	})
   732â†’	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
   733â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   734â†’
   735â†’	allInsts := walker.InstanceExpander.AllInstances()
   736â†’
   737â†’	moveValidateDiags := c.postPlanValidateMoves(config, moveStmts, allInsts)
   738â†’	if moveValidateDiags.HasErrors() {
   739â†’		// If any of the move statements are invalid then those errors take
   740â†’		// precedence over any other errors because an incomplete move graph
   741â†’		// is quite likely to be the _cause_ of various errors. This oddity
   742â†’		// comes from the fact that we need to apply the moves before we
   743â†’		// actually validate them, because validation depends on the result
   744â†’		// of first trying to plan.
   745â†’		return nil, nil, moveValidateDiags
   746â†’	}
   747â†’	diags = diags.Append(moveValidateDiags) // might just contain warnings
   748â†’
   749â†’	if moveResults.Blocked.Len() > 0 && !diags.HasErrors() {
   750â†’		// If we had blocked moves and we're not going to be returning errors
   751â†’		// then we'll report the blockers as a warning. We do this only in the
   752â†’		// absense of errors because invalid move statements might well be
   753â†’		// the root cause of the blockers, and so better to give an actionable
   754â†’		// error message than a less-actionable warning.
   755â†’		diags = diags.Append(blockedMovesWarningDiag(moveResults))
   756â†’	}
   757â†’
   758â†’	// If we reach this point with error diagnostics then "changes" is a
   759â†’	// representation of the subset of changes we were able to plan before
   760â†’	// we encountered errors, which we'll return as part of a non-nil plan
   761â†’	// so that e.g. the UI can show what was planned so far in case that extra
   762â†’	// context helps the user to understand the error messages we're returning.
   763â†’	prevRunState = walker.PrevRunState.Close()
   764â†’
   765â†’	// The refreshed state may have data resource objects which were deferred
   766â†’	// to apply and cannot be serialized.
   767â†’	walker.RefreshState.RemovePlannedResourceInstanceObjects()
   768â†’	priorState := walker.RefreshState.Close()
   769â†’
   770â†’	driftedResources, driftDiags := c.driftedResources(config, prevRunState, priorState, moveResults)
   771â†’	diags = diags.Append(driftDiags)
   772â†’
   773â†’	deferredResources, deferredDiags := c.deferredResources(config, walker.Deferrals.GetDeferredChanges(), priorState)
   774â†’	diags = diags.Append(deferredDiags)
   775â†’
   776â†’	var forgottenResources []string
   777â†’	for _, rc := range changes.Resources {
   778â†’		if rc.Action == plans.Forget {
   779â†’			// TODO KEM display resource ids
   780â†’			forgottenResources = append(forgottenResources, fmt.Sprintf(" - %s", rc.Addr))
   781â†’		}
   782â†’	}
   783â†’	if len(forgottenResources) > 0 {
   784â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   785â†’			tfdiags.Warning,
   786â†’			"Some objects will no longer be managed by Terraform",
   787â†’			fmt.Sprintf("If you apply this plan, Terraform will discard its tracking information for the following objects, but it will not delete them:\n%s\n\nAfter applying this plan, Terraform will no longer manage these objects. You will need to import them into Terraform to manage them again.", strings.Join(forgottenResources, "\n")),
   788â†’		))
   789â†’	}
   790â†’	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, prevRunState)
   791â†’	// We must finish building a plan object, and cannot return early here.
   792â†’	var changesSrc *plans.ChangesSrc
   793â†’	var err error
   794â†’	if !schemaDiags.HasErrors() {
   795â†’		changesSrc, err = changes.Encode(schemas)
   796â†’		if err != nil {
   797â†’			diags = diags.Append(err)
   798â†’		}
   799â†’	}
   800â†’
   801â†’	plan := &plans.Plan{
   802â†’		UIMode:                  opts.Mode,
   803â†’		Changes:                 changesSrc,
   804â†’		DriftedResources:        driftedResources,
   805â†’		DeferredResources:       deferredResources,
   806â†’		PrevRunState:            prevRunState,
   807â†’		PriorState:              priorState,
   808â†’		ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   809â†’		Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   810â†’		Checks:                  states.NewCheckResults(walker.Checks),
   811â†’		Timestamp:               timestamp,
   812â†’		ProviderFunctionResults: providerFuncResults.GetHashes(),
   813â†’
   814â†’		// Other fields get populated by Context.Plan after we return
   815â†’	}
   816â†’
   817â†’	// Our final rulings on whether the plan is "complete" and "applyable".
   818â†’	// See the documentation for these plan fields to learn what exactly they
   819â†’	// are intended to mean.
   820â†’	if !diags.HasErrors() {
   821â†’		if len(opts.Targets) == 0 && !walker.Deferrals.HaveAnyDeferrals() {
   822â†’			// A plan without any targets or deferred actions should be
   823â†’			// complete if we didn't encounter errors while producing it.
   824â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is complete")
   825â†’			plan.Complete = true
   826â†’		} else {
   827â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is incomplete")
   828â†’		}
   829â†’		if opts.Mode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   830â†’			// In refresh-only mode we explicitly don't expect to propose any
   831â†’			// actions, but the plan is applyable if the state was changed
   832â†’			// in an interesting way by the refresh step.
   833â†’			plan.Applyable = !plan.PriorState.ManagedResourcesEqual(plan.PrevRunState) || !plan.PriorState.RootOutputValuesEqual(plan.PrevRunState)
   834â†’		} else {
   835â†’			// For other planning modes a plan is applyable if its "changes"
   836â†’			// are not considered empty (by whatever rules the plans package
   837â†’			// uses to decide that).
   838â†’			plan.Applyable = !plan.Changes.Empty()
   839â†’		}
   840â†’		if plan.Applyable {
   841â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is applyable")
   842â†’		} else {
   843â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is not applyable")
   844â†’		}
   845â†’	} else {
   846â†’		log.Println("[WARN] Planning encountered errors, so plan is not applyable")
   847â†’	}
   848â†’
   849â†’	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
   850â†’	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
   851â†’	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
   852â†’	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   853â†’
   854â†’	return plan, evalScope, diags
   855â†’}

   857â†’func (c *Context) deferredResources(config *configs.Config, deferrals []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChange, state *states.State) ([]*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   858â†’	var deferredResources []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc
   859â†’
   860â†’	schemas, diags := c.Schemas(config, state)
   861â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   862â†’		return deferredResources, diags
   863â†’	}
   864â†’
   865â†’	for _, deferral := range deferrals {
   866â†’
   867â†’		schema, _ := schemas.ResourceTypeConfig(
   868â†’			deferral.Change.ProviderAddr.Provider,
   869â†’			deferral.Change.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode,
   870â†’			deferral.Change.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type)
   871â†’
   872â†’		ty := schema.ImpliedType()
   873â†’		deferralSrc, err := deferral.Encode(ty)
   874â†’		if err != nil {
   875â†’			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   876â†’				tfdiags.Error,
   877â†’				"Failed to prepare deferred resource for plan",
   878â†’				fmt.Sprintf("The deferred resource %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", deferral.Change.Addr, err)))
   879â†’			continue
   880â†’		}
   881â†’
   882â†’		deferredResources = append(deferredResources, deferralSrc)
   883â†’	}
   884â†’	return deferredResources, diags
   885â†’}

   887â†’func (c *Context) planGraph(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   888â†’	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   889â†’	if opts != nil {
   890â†’		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   891â†’	}
   892â†’
   893â†’	switch mode := opts.Mode; mode {
   894â†’	case plans.NormalMode:
   895â†’		// In Normal mode we need to pay attention to import and removed blocks
   896â†’		// in config so their targets can be added to the graph.
   897â†’		forgetResources, forgetModules, diags := c.findForgetTargets(config)
   898â†’		if diags.HasErrors() {
   899â†’			return nil, walkPlan, diags
   900â†’		}
   901â†’		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   902â†’			Config:                  config,
   903â†’			State:                   prevRunState,
   904â†’			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   905â†’			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   906â†’			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   907â†’			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   908â†’			ForceReplace:            opts.ForceReplace,
   909â†’			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   910â†’			preDestroyRefresh:       opts.PreDestroyRefresh,
   911â†’			Operation:               walkPlan,
   912â†’			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   913â†’			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   914â†’			ImportTargets:           c.findImportTargets(config),
   915â†’			forgetResources:         forgetResources,
   916â†’			forgetModules:           forgetModules,
   917â†’			GenerateConfigPath:      opts.GenerateConfigPath,
   918â†’		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   919â†’		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   920â†’	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   921â†’		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   922â†’			Config:                  config,
   923â†’			State:                   prevRunState,
   924â†’			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   925â†’			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   926â†’			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   927â†’			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   928â†’			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   929â†’			skipPlanChanges:         true, // this activates "refresh only" mode.
   930â†’			Operation:               walkPlan,
   931â†’			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   932â†’			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   933â†’		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   934â†’		return graph, walkPlan, diags

   140â†’// Plan generates an execution plan by comparing the given configuration
   141â†’// with the given previous run state.
   142â†’//
   143â†’// The given planning options allow control of various other details of the
   144â†’// planning process that are not represented directly in the configuration.
   145â†’// You can use terraform.DefaultPlanOpts to generate a normal plan with no
   146â†’// special options.

   149â†’// applyable, although Terraform cannot guarantee that applying it will fully
   150â†’// succeed. If the returned diagnostics contains errors but this method
   151â†’// still returns a non-nil Plan then the plan describes the subset of actions
   152â†’// planned so far, which is not safe to apply but could potentially be used
   153â†’// by the UI layer to give extra context to support understanding of the
   154â†’// returned error messages.
   155â†’func (c *Context) Plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {

   162â†’// root module based on the content of the generated plan.

   164â†’// The scope will be nil if the planning process doesn't complete successfully
   165â†’// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned plan is nil
   166â†’// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
   167â†’// to be nil even when the plan isn't, if the plan is not complete enough for
   168â†’// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.

If the returned diagnostics contains errors but this method\n// still returns a non-nil Plan then the plan describes the subset of actions\n// planned so far, which is not safe to apply but could potentially be used\n// by the UI layer to give extra context to support understanding of the\n// returned error messages.\nfunc (c *Context) Plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tplan, _, diags := c.PlanAndEval(config, prevRunState, opts)\n\treturn plan, diags\n}\n\n// PlanAndEval is like [Context.Plan] except that it additionally makes a\n// best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the\n// root module based on the content of the generated plan.\n//\n// The scope will be nil if the planning process doesn't complete successfully\n// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned plan is nil\n// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope\n// to be nil even when the plan isn't, if the plan is not complete enough for\n// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.\nfunc (c *Context) PlanAndEval(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tdefer c.acquireRun(\"plan\")()\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// Save the downstream functions from needing to deal with these broken situations.\n\t// No real callers should rely on these, but we have a bunch of old and\n\t// sloppy tests that don't always populate arguments properly.\n\tif config == nil {\n\t\tconfig = configs.NewEmptyConfig()\n\t}\n\tif prevRunState == nil {\n\t\tprevRunState = states.NewState()\n\t}\n\tif opts == nil {\n\t\topts = &PlanOpts{\n\t\t\tMode: plans.NormalMode,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmoreDiags := c.checkConfigDependencies(config)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\tmoreDiags = c.checkStateDependencies(prevRunState)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\n\t// If required dependencies are not available then we'll bail early since\n\t// otherwise we're likely to just see a bunch of other errors related to\n\t// incompatibilities, which could be overwhelming for the user.\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tproviderCfgDiags := checkExternalProviders(config, nil, prevRunState, opts.ExternalProviders)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(providerCfgDiags)\n\tif providerCfgDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tswitch opts.Mode {\n\tcase plans.NormalMode, plans.DestroyMode:\n\t\t// OK\n\tcase plans.RefreshOnlyMode:\n\t\tif opts.SkipRefresh {\n\t\t\t// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should prevent this\n\t\t\t// combination of options.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Incompatible plan options\",\n\t\t\t\t\"Cannot skip refreshing in refresh-only mode. This is a bug in Terraform.\",\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should not try to\n\t\t// create a context for a mode that Terraform Core doesn't support.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Unsupported plan mode\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform Core doesn't know how to handle plan mode %s. This is a bug in Terraform.\", opts.Mode),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\tif len(opts.ForceReplace) > 0 && opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {\n\t\t// The other modes don't generate no-op or update actions that we might\n\t\t// upgrade to be \"replace\", so doesn't make sense to combine those.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Unsupported plan mode\",\n\t\t\t\"Forcing resource instance replacement (with -replace=...) is allowed only in normal planning mode.\",\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\tif opts.Forget && opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Unsupported plan mode\",\n\t\t\t\"Forgetting all resources is only allowed in the context of a destroy plan. This is a bug in Terraform, please report it.\",\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// By the time we get here, we should have values defined for all of\n\t// the root module variables, even if some of them are \"unknown\". It's the\n\t// caller's responsibility to have already handled the decoding of these\n\t// from the various ways the CLI allows them to be set and to produce\n\t// user-friendly error messages if they are not all present, and so\n\t// the error message from checkInputVariables should never be seen and\n\t// includes language asking the user to report a bug.\n\tvarDiags := checkInputVariables(config.Module.Variables, opts.SetVariables)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(varDiags)\n\n\tif len(opts.Targets) > 0 {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\t\t\t\"Resource targeting is in effect\",\n\t\t\t`You are creating a plan with the -target option, which means that the result of this plan may not represent all of the changes requested by the current configuration.\n\nThe -target option is not for routine use, and is provided only for exceptional situations such as recovering from errors or mistakes, or when Terraform specifically suggests to use it as part of an error message.`,\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\n\tvar plan *plans.Plan\n\tvar planDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar evalScope *lang.Scope\n\tswitch opts.Mode {\n\tcase plans.NormalMode:\n\t\tplan, evalScope, planDiags = c.plan(config, prevRunState, opts)\n\tcase plans.DestroyMode:\n\t\tplan, evalScope, planDiags = c.destroyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)\n\tcase plans.RefreshOnlyMode:\n\t\tplan, evalScope, planDiags = c.refreshOnlyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unsupported plan mode %s\", opts.Mode))\n\t}\n\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n\t// NOTE: We're intentionally not returning early when diags.HasErrors\n\t// here because we'll still populate other metadata below on a best-effort\n\t// basis to try to give the UI some extra context to return alongside the\n\t// error messages.\n", "numLines": 150, "startLine": 138, "totalLines": 1188}}

   413â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   414â†’	if opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
   415â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.plan with %s", opts.Mode))
   416â†’	}
   417â†’
   418â†’	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
   419â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   420â†’

   424â†’func (c *Context) refreshOnlyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   425â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   426â†’
   427â†’	if opts.Mode != plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   428â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.refreshOnlyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
   429â†’	}
   430â†’
   431â†’	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
   432â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   433â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   434â†’		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   435â†’		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   436â†’		// to help understand the errors.
   437â†’		return plan, evalScope, diags
   438â†’	}
   439â†’
   440â†’	// If the graph builder and graph nodes correctly obeyed our directive
   441â†’	// to refresh only, the set of resource changes should always be empty.
   442â†’	// We'll safety-check that here so we can return a clear message about it,
   443â†’	// rather than probably just generating confusing output at the UI layer.
   444â†’	if len(plan.Changes.Resources) != 0 {
   445â†’		// Some extra context in the logs in case the user reports this message
   446â†’		// as a bug, as a starting point for debugging.
   447â†’		for _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {
   448â†’			if depKey := rc.DeposedKey; depKey == states.NotDeposed {
   449â†’				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr)
   450â†’			} else {
   451â†’				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s deposed object %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr, depKey)
   452â†’			}
   453â†’		}
   454â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   455â†’			tfdiags.Error,
   456â†’			"Invalid refresh-only plan",
   457â†’			"Terraform generated planned resource changes in a refresh-only plan. This is a bug in Terraform.",
   458â†’		))
   459â†’	}
   460â†’
   461â†’	// We don't populate RelevantResources for a refresh-only plan, because
   462â†’	// they never have any planned actions and so no resource can ever be
   463â†’	// "relevant" per the intended meaning of that field.
   464â†’
   465â†’	return plan, evalScope, diags
   466â†’}

   468â†’func (c *Context) destroyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   469â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   470â†’
   471â†’	if opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
   472â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.destroyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
   473â†’	}
   474â†’
   475â†’	priorState := prevRunState
   476â†’
   477â†’	// A destroy plan starts by running Refresh to read any pending data
   478â†’	// sources, and remove missing managed resources. This is required because
   479â†’	// a "destroy plan" is only creating delete changes, and is essentially a
   480â†’	// local operation.
   481â†’	//
   482â†’	// NOTE: if skipRefresh _is_ set then we'll rely on the destroy-plan walk
   483â†’	// below to upgrade the prevRunState and priorState both to the latest
   484â†’	// resource type schemas, so NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance.Execute
   485â†’	// must coordinate with this by taking that action only when c.skipRefresh
   486â†’	// _is_ set. This coupling between the two is unfortunate but necessary
   487â†’	// to work within our current structure.
   488â†’	if !opts.SkipRefresh && !prevRunState.Empty() {
   489â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: calling Context.plan to get the effect of refreshing the prior state")
   490â†’		refreshOpts := *opts
   491â†’		refreshOpts.Mode = plans.NormalMode
   492â†’		refreshOpts.PreDestroyRefresh = true
   493â†’
   494â†’		// FIXME: A normal plan is required here to refresh the state, because
   495â†’		// the state and configuration may not match during a destroy, and a
   496â†’		// normal refresh plan can fail with evaluation errors. In the future
   497â†’		// the destroy plan should take care of refreshing instances itself,
   498â†’		// where the special cases of evaluation and skipping condition checks
   499â†’		// can be done.
   500â†’		refreshPlan, _, refreshDiags := c.plan(config, prevRunState, &refreshOpts)
   501â†’		if refreshDiags.HasErrors() {
   502â†’			// NOTE: Normally we'd append diagnostics regardless of whether
   503â†’			// there are errors, just in case there are warnings we'd want to
   504â†’			// preserve, but we're intentionally _not_ doing that here because
   505â†’			// if the first plan succeeded then we'll be running another plan
   506â†’			// in DestroyMode below, and we don't want to double-up any
   507â†’			// warnings that both plan walks would generate.
   508â†’			// (This does mean we won't show any warnings that would've been
   509â†’			// unique to only this walk, but we're assuming here that if the
   510â†’			// warnings aren't also applicable to a destroy plan then we'd
   511â†’			// rather not show them here, because this non-destroy plan for
   512â†’			// refreshing is largely an implementation detail.)
   513â†’			diags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)
   514â†’			return nil, nil, diags
   515â†’		}
   516â†’
   517â†’		// We'll use the refreshed state -- which is the  "prior state" from
   518â†’		// the perspective of this "destroy plan" -- as the starting state
   519â†’		// for our destroy-plan walk, so it can take into account if we
   520â†’		// detected during refreshing that anything was already deleted outside
   521â†’		// of Terraform.
   522â†’		priorState = refreshPlan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
   523â†’
   524â†’		// The refresh plan may have upgraded state for some resources, make
   525â†’		// sure we store the new version.
   526â†’		prevRunState = refreshPlan.PrevRunState.DeepCopy()
   527â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: now _really_ creating a destroy plan")
   528â†’	}
   529â†’
   530â†’	destroyPlan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, priorState, opts)
   531â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   532â†’	if walkDiags.HasErrors() {
   533â†’		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
   534â†’		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
   535â†’		// to help understand the errors.
   536â†’		return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
   537â†’	}
   538â†’
   539â†’	if !opts.SkipRefresh {
   540â†’		// If we didn't skip refreshing then we want the previous run state to
   541â†’		// be the one we originally fed into the c.refreshOnlyPlan call above,
   542â†’		// not the refreshed version we used for the destroy planWalk.
   543â†’		destroyPlan.PrevRunState = prevRunState
   544â†’	}
   545â†’
   546â†’	relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, destroyPlan)
   547â†’	diags = diags.Append(rDiags)
   548â†’
   549â†’	destroyPlan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs

   838â†’			plan.Applyable = !plan.Changes.Empty()

   843â†’			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is not applyable")
   844â†’		}

    19â†’	if config != nil {
    20â†’		configBody = config.Config
    21â†’	}
    22â†’
    23â†’	var inputBody hcl.Body
    24â†’	inputConfig := ctx.ProviderInput(addr)
    25â†’	if len(inputConfig) > 0 {
    26â†’		inputBody = configs.SynthBody("<input-prompt>", inputConfig)
    27â†’	}
    28â†’
    29â†’	switch {
    30â†’	case configBody != nil && inputBody != nil:
    31â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: merging explicit config and input", addr)
    32â†’		return hcl.MergeBodies([]hcl.Body{inputBody, configBody})
    33â†’	case configBody != nil:
    34â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: using explicit config only", addr)
    35â†’		return configBody
    36â†’	case inputBody != nil:
    37â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: using input only", addr)
    38â†’		return inputBody
    39â†’	default:
    40â†’		log.Printf("[TRACE] buildProviderConfig for %s: no configuration at all", addr)
    41â†’		return hcl.EmptyBody()
    42â†’	}
    43â†’}
    44â†’
    45â†’// getProvider returns the providers.Interface and schema for a given provider.
    46â†’func getProvider(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) (providers.Interface, providers.ProviderSchema, error) {
    47â†’	if addr.Provider.Type == "" {
    48â†’		// Should never happen
    49â†’		panic("GetProvider used with uninitialized provider configuration address")
    50â†’	}
    51â†’	provider := ctx.Provider(addr)
    52â†’	if provider == nil {
    53â†’		return nil, providers.ProviderSchema{}, fmt.Errorf("provider %s not initialized", addr)
    54â†’	}
    55â†’	// Not all callers require a schema, so we will leave checking for a nil
    56â†’	// schema to the callers.
    57â†’	schema, err := ctx.ProviderSchema(addr)
    58â†’	if err != nil {
    59â†’		return nil, providers.ProviderSchema{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to read schema for provider %s: %w", addr, err)
    60â†’	}
    61â†’	return provider, schema, nil
    62â†’}

    27â†’)
    28â†’
    29â†’// NodePlannableResourceInstance represents a _single_ resource
    30â†’// instance that is plannable. This means this represents a single
    31â†’// count index, for example.
    32â†’type NodePlannableResourceInstance struct {
    33â†’	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance
    34â†’	ForceCreateBeforeDestroy bool
    35â†’
    36â†’	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances
    37â†’	skipRefresh bool
    38â†’
    39â†’	// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions
    40â†’	// for any instances.
    41â†’	skipPlanChanges bool
    42â†’
    43â†’	// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to
    44â†’	// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so
    45â†’	// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource
    46â†’	// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.
    47â†’	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    48â†’
    49â†’	// replaceTriggeredBy stores references from replace_triggered_by which
    50â†’	// triggered this instance to be replaced.
    51â†’	replaceTriggeredBy []*addrs.Reference
    52â†’
    53â†’	// importTarget, if populated, contains the information necessary to plan
    54â†’	// an import of this resource.
    55â†’	importTarget cty.Value
    56â†’}
    57â†’
    58â†’var (
    59â†’	_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    60â†’	_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    61â†’	_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    62â†’	_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    63â†’	_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    64â†’	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    65â†’	_ GraphNodeAttachResourceState  = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    66â†’	_ GraphNodeExecutable           = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)
    67â†’)
    68â†’
    69â†’// GraphNodeEvalable
    70â†’func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
    71â†’	addr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()
    72â†’
    73â†’	// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is
    74â†’	switch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {
    75â†’	case addrs.ManagedResourceMode:
    76â†’		return n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)
    77â†’	case addrs.DataResourceMode:
    78â†’		return n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)
    79â†’	case addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:
    80â†’		return n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)
    81â†’	default:
    82â†’		panic(fmt.Errorf("unsupported resource mode %s", n.Config.Mode))
    83â†’	}
    84â†’}
    85â†’
    86â†’func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    87â†’	config := n.Config
    88â†’	addr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()
    89â†’
    90â†’	var change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange
    91â†’
    92â†’	_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)
    93â†’	diags = diags.Append(err)
    94â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
    95â†’		return diags
    96â†’	}
    97â†’
    98â†’	diags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))
    99â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   100â†’		return diags
   101â†’	}
   102â†’
   103â†’	checkRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error
   104â†’	if n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {
   105â†’		checkRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning
   106â†’	}
   107â†’
   108â†’	deferrals := ctx.Deferrals()
   109â†’	change, state, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.planDataSource(ctx, checkRuleSeverity, n.skipPlanChanges, deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, n.Dependencies))
   110â†’	diags = diags.Append(planDiags)
   111â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   112â†’		return diags
   113â†’	}
   114â†’
   115â†’	// A nil change here indicates that Terraform is deciding NOT to make a
   116â†’	// change at all. In which case even if we wanted to try and defer it
   117â†’	// (because of a dependency) we can't as there is no change to defer.
   118â†’	//
   119â†’	// The most common case for this is when the data source is being refreshed
   120â†’	// but depends on unknown values or dependencies which means we just skip
   121â†’	// refreshing the data source. We maintain that behaviour here.
   122â†’	if change != nil && deferred != nil {
   123â†’		// Then this data source got deferred by the provider during planning.
   124â†’		deferrals.ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)
   125â†’	} else {
   126â†’		// Not deferred; business as usual.
   127â†’
   128â†’		// write the data source into both the refresh state and the
   129â†’		// working state
   130â†’		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))
   131â†’		if diags.HasErrors() {
   132â†’			return diags
   133â†’		}
   134â†’		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))
   135â†’		if diags.HasErrors() {
   136â†’			return diags
   137â†’		}
   138â†’
   139â†’		diags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, ""))
   140â†’
   141â†’		// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this
   142â†’		// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against
   143â†’		// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations
   144â†’		// until the user makes the condition succeed.
   145â†’		checkDiags := evalCheckRules(
   146â†’			addrs.ResourcePostcondition,
   147â†’			n.Config.Postconditions,
   148â†’			ctx, addr, repeatData,
   149â†’			checkRuleSeverity,
   150â†’		)

    29â†’// NodePlannableResourceInstance represents a _single_ resource
    30â†’// instance that is plannable. This means this represents a single
    31â†’// count index, for example.
    32â†’type NodePlannableResourceInstance struct {
    33â†’	*NodeAbstractResourceInstance
    34â†’	ForceCreateBeforeDestroy bool
    35â†’
    36â†’	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances
    37â†’	skipRefresh bool
    38â†’
    39â†’	// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions
    40â†’	// for any instances.
    41â†’	skipPlanChanges bool
    42â†’
    43â†’	// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to
    44â†’	// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so
    45â†’	// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource
    46â†’	// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.
    47â†’	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    48â†’
    49â†’	// replaceTriggeredBy stores references from replace_triggered_by which
    50â†’	// triggered this instance to be replaced.
    51â†’	replaceTriggeredBy []*addrs.Reference
    52â†’
    53â†’	// importTarget, if populated, contains the information necessary to plan
    54â†’	// an import of this resource.
    55â†’	importTarget cty.Value
    56â†’}

    71â†’	addr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()

    74â†’	switch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {
    75â†’	case addrs.ManagedResourceMode:

    77â†’	case addrs.DataResourceMode:

    83â†’	}
    84â†’}
